title: Identify a self-contradiction: async: true + mode(:shared)
prompt: The fixture at `test_fixtures/async_shared_contradiction.ex` declares `use MyApp.DataCase, async: true` AND calls `Ecto.Adapters.SQL.Sandbox.mode(MyApp.Repo, {:shared, self()})` in setup. Rewrite to `test/my_app/report_generator_test.exs` removing the mode(:shared) call — preserve `async: true` because the test does not spawn any child processes that need shared mode. Add a comment in the setup block: `# async: true implies manual mode — no mode/2 call needed`.
tier_rationale: Requires recognizing the self-contradiction, keeping async, and removing the offending line.
research_citation: "you are using the code above and also setting `async: true`. This means the sandbox won't enter shared mode" — Elixir Forum, joey_the_snake
